summary: Fire‑filled set with Dark Star, China>Rider and strong second half
blurb: The Boston Garden show delivers a scorching second set highlighted by Dark Star, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a punchy New Speedway Boogie. Jerry’s guitar soars after a slower first set, while Bruce Hornsby adds lush keyboards throughout.
review:
On September 24, 1991 the Dead took Boston Garden by storm. The first set opened with solid roots material—Let The Good Times Roll and Althea—but it was the second half that truly ignited the crowd. A blistering China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue showcased Jerry’s “crazy midi‑fied” solos, and the ensuing Dark Star unfolded with relentless energy. Highlights continued with Ship Of Fools, Foolish Heart, and a soaring Standing On The Moon that rescued any early‑set sluggishness. The night closed on a high note with New Speedway Boogie and a heartfelt Weight encore.

Bruce Hornsby’s keyboard textures are repeatedly praised for their “psychedelic bayou” flavor, though one listener noted Vince Welnick’s playing as overly cheesy. Overall the show is considered one of the stronger entries in the 1991 Boston run, especially for fans chasing that fiery second‑set vibe.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of eight recordings are positive, repeatedly praising Dark Star, China>Rider and the second‑set energy; mixed comments only note a slower first set or one poor keyboard performance, yielding strong overall sentiment but not flawless unanimity.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard mix, good remaster, and captures the fire of the Boston run with standout China>Rider, Dark Star and Desolation Row.

key_highlights: Scorching second set with Dark Star and high‑energy segues
Bruce Hornsby’s lush keyboard textures elevate the performance
New Speedway Boogie closes the first set on a triumphant note

band_performance.Jerry: Starts a bit sluggish in the first set, but his guitar erupts on China>Rider and Dark Star with wild, midi‑fied solos; lyric delivery shines on Standing On The Moon.
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby (keyboards) is praised for adding a psychedelic bayou texture that drives the show forward; Vince Welnick receives a lone negative comment describing his playing as cheesy and overwhelming.
